calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For Release Of Escheated Property Over $60,000
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Off Calendar

PETITION Petition For Release Of Escheated Property Over $60,000
Off Calendar Mar-10-2010 Continued To May-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Harold Paton (Petitioner). Susan Kawala from the Attorney General's Office for the State Controller's Office.

Counsel stated that they would be exchanging information and have further discussions. Counsel also presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the name change issue. The Court stated that all issues needed to be briefed and that the preliminary issue needed to be presented. The petition with regard to the name change issue is to be filed by April 26, 2010. A response is due by May 10, 2010. A reply is due by May 17, 2010. The matter was continued to May 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of the petition regarding the name change issue.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION Petition For Release Of Escheated Property Over $60,000

PETITION Petition For Release Of Escheated Property Over $60,000
Off Calendar Dec-02-2009 Continued To Jan-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Release Of Escheated Property Over $60,000
Off Calendar Sep-23-2009 Continued To Dec-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Kawala for the State Controllers Office. John Boessenecker specially appearing for Ewald Schlachter for Harold Patton, Jr.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that Mr. Schlachter was in the hospital, that Mr. Schlachter wanted the matter dropped off today's calendar and put over to a later date. Ms. Kawala stated that she did not have a problem with putting the matter over and requested a reply be filed prior to the next hearing. The matter was continued to December 2, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Mr. Schlachter's reply is due by November 10, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
